edges.description: Premature high-amplitude pulsatile GnRH secretion activates pituitary gonadotrophs, driving early HPG axis activation and premature gonadarche.
edges.source: Premature Reactivation of Pulsatile GnRH Secretion
edges.target: Early HPG Axis Activation and Premature Gonadarche

edges.description: Premature gonadarche and sex-steroid exposure produce early onset of secondary sexual characteristics (precocious puberty).
edges.source: Early HPG Axis Activation and Premature Gonadarche

edges.description: Early sex-steroid exposure advances skeletal maturation/bone age, which can compromise adult height.
edges.source: Early HPG Axis Activation and Premature Gonadarche

edges.description: Sex-steroid-driven growth acceleration produces transiently increased height velocity and tall stature for age in childhood.
edges.source: Early HPG Axis Activation and Premature Gonadarche

edges.target: Premature Reactivation of Pulsatile GnRH Secretion

nodes.description: Sex-steroid exposure accelerates bone maturation, advancing bone age relative to chronological age and risking premature epiphyseal fusion and reduced adult height.

Premature GnRH pulsatility activates pituitary gonadotrophs to secrete LH and FSH, which stimulate the gonads to produce sex steroids (estradiol in girls, testosterone in boys). The resulting premature gonadarche drives early development of secondary sexual characteristics, accelerated linear growth, and advanced skeletal maturation that can compromise adult height.
nodes.id: Early HPG Axis Activation and Premature Gonadarche

evidence.reference_title: Central precocious puberty: From genetics to treatment.
evidence.snippet:
Central precocious puberty (CPP) results from early activation of the 
hypothalamic - pituitary -gonadal (HPG) axis and follows the same sequence as 
normal puberty.

nodes.description: Long-acting GnRH agonists (e.g., leuprolide) are first-line therapy for CPP. Continuous (non-pulsatile) GnRH receptor stimulation desensitizes pituitary gonadotrophs, suppressing LH/FSH and sex-steroid secretion, halting pubertal progression and preserving adult height potential.

evidence.reference_title: Central precocious puberty: From genetics to treatment.
evidence.snippet:
GnRH analogs are the mainstay of treatment and are used 
with the goal of height preservation.

nodes.description: Onset of secondary sexual characteristics before age 8 in girls and 9 in boys, driven by GnRH-dependent activation of the HPG axis.

evidence.reference_title: A GPR54-activating mutation in a patient with central precocious puberty.
evidence.snippet:
Gonadotropin-dependent, or central, precocious puberty is caused by early 
maturation of the hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis. In girls, this condition 
is most often idiopathic.

nodes.description: The central event in CPP is premature reawakening of the hypothalamic GnRH pulse generator. Hypothalamic GnRH neurons resume high-amplitude pulsatile GnRH secretion before the normal age of puberty, driving pituitary gonadotropin release. Upstream kisspeptin (KISS1/KISS1R) signaling stimulates GnRH neurons, while inhibitory factors including the paternally expressed imprinted genes MKRN3 and DLK1 normally restrain the axis during childhood; loss of this restraint or gain of kisspeptin tone triggers premature secretion.
nodes.id: Premature Reactivation of Pulsatile GnRH Secretion

evidence.reference_title: Central precocious puberty caused by mutations in the imprinted gene MKRN3.
evidence.snippet:
The onset of puberty is first detected as an increase in pulsatile 
secretion of gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH). Early activation of the 
hypothalamic-pituitary-gonadal axis results in central precocious puberty.

nodes.description: Sex-steroid-driven growth acceleration produces tall stature relative to peers during childhood, although early epiphyseal fusion may compromise final adult height.
